content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 16, 1990


David Lee, Esq.
Treasurer
Committee to Elect Darlene Lee
4793 Ewing Road
Castro Valley, CA  94546

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-214

Dear Mr. Lee:

You are seeking advice on behalf of the Committee to Elect
Darlene Lee ("the committee") regarding the permissible uses of
campaign funds under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 As
treasurer for this committee we provide you advice pursuant
to Regulation 18329 (copy enclosed); we are treating your question
as a request for informal assistance because your request for ad-
vices does not refer to a specific pending governmental decision
(Regulation 18329.)

The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of March 11, 1990.

QUESTION

Does the Act permit the committee to make donations of its
campaign funds to nonprofit organizations?

CONCLUSION

The committee can make donations of its campaign funds to
nonprofit organizations provided certain requirements are met.


___________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-90-214
Page 2

FACTS

The Committee to Elect Darlene Lee, formed in 1984, was
Mrs. Lee's campaign committee for election to a seat on the Eden
Hospital Board of Directors ("the board") in the November 1984
election. Mrs. Lee, currently a member of the board, was first
elected in 1984 and subsequently reelected in 1988.

The committee has not raised any campaign funds since 1984,
and now wishes to make donations to nonprofit organizations.

ANALYSIS

The Act permits campaign funds to be use to make donations to
nonprofit organizations, provided three requirements are met.
First, the recipient organization must be a bona fide charitable,
educational civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt nonprofit
organization. Second, no substantial part of the proceeds of the do-
donation can have a material financial effect on the candidate or
elected officer, members of his or her immediate family, or the
treasurer of the committee. Third, the donation must bear a rea-
sonable relationship to political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Section 85803.)

Because your letter has not provided any information with
respect to the circumstances of the contemplated donation by the
committee, whether the donated funds will not be used by the
recipient nonprofit organizations to support or oppose candidates
or ballot measures, we can only provide you with general advice.

With respect to the first requirement, you can verify with
the entities designated to receive the contemplated donations from
the committee to determine whether they are qualifying nonprofit
organizations.

With respect to the second requirement, no substantial part
of the proceeds of the donation can have a material financial ef-
fect on the candidate or elected officer, members of his or her
immediate family, or the treasurer of the committee. We have pre-
viously advised that the test to determine whether the material
effect result is that set forth in subdivision (a)(4) of
Regulation 18702.1 (copy enclosed). That section provides:

The decision will result in the personal
expenses, income, assets (other than interest in
real property), or liabilities of the official or
his or her immediate family increasing or decreas-
ing by at least $250.

Therefore, if an increase or decrease of at least $250 in the per-
sonal expenses, income, assets or liabilities of Mrs. Lee, her
immediate family, or of the committee's treasurer will result from

File No. I-90-214
Page 3

the donation, the campaign funds may not be given. (See Statham
Advice Letter, I-89-690, copy enclosed.)

With respect to the third requirement, whether the donations
bear a reasonable relation to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose would depend on the specific facts of the donor
elected official's situation and the recipient of the donation.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSR:plh

Enclosures

sections.conclusion:
The committee can make donations of its campaign funds to nonprofit organizations provided certain requirements are met.

sections.facts:
The Committee to Elect Darlene Lee, formed in 1984, was Mrs. Lee's campaign committee for election to a seat on the Eden Hospital Board of Directors ("the board") in the November 1984 election. Mrs. Lee, currently a member of the board, was first elected in 1984 and subsequently reelected in 1988.

The committee has not raised any campaign funds since 1984, and now wishes to make donations to nonprofit organizations.
sections.analysis:
The Act permits campaign funds to be used to make donations to nonprofit organizations, provided three requirements are met. First, the recipient organization must be a bona fide charitable, educational, civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt nonprofit organization. Second, no substantial part of the proceeds of the donation can have a material financial effect on the candidate or elected officer, members of his or her immediate family, or the treasurer of the committee. Third, the donation must bear a reasonable relationship to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 85803.)

Because your letter has not provided any information with respect to the circumstances of the contemplated donation by the committee, other than the donated funds will not be used by the recipient nonprofit organizations to support or oppose candidates or ballot measures, we can only provide you with general advice.

With respect to the first requirement, you can verify with the entities designated to receive the contemplated donations from the committee to determine whether they are qualifying nonprofit organizations.

With respect to the second requirement, no substantial part of the proceeds of the donation can have a material financial effect on the candidate or elected officer, members of his or her immediate family, or the treasurer of the committee. We have previously advised that the test to determine whether the requisite material effect result is that set forth in subdivision (a)(4) of Regulation 18702.1 (copy enclosed). That section provides:

The decision will result in the personal expenses, income, assets (other than interest in real property), or liabilities of the official or his or her immediate family increasing or decreasing by at least $250.

Therefore, if an increase or decrease of at least $250 in the personal expenses, income, assets or liabilities of Mrs. Lee, her immediate family, or of the committee's treasurer will result from

the donation, the campaign funds may not be given. (See Statham Advice Letter, I-89-690, copy enclosed.)

With respect to the third requirement, whether the donations bear a reasonable relation to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose would depend on the specific facts of the donor elected official's situation and the recipient of the donation.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter please contact me at (916) 322-5091.
